Advisor Notations
1. Request id 0000013777 Test Credit. Permission to apply 32 credits from AP Exam. A. Adviser  09/17/2024
 
Courses Taken Outside of the Undergraduate Career (RG5000)
Courses Taken Outside of the Undergraduate Career
 
 
Courses Taken Outside of the Undergraduate Career (R1659/10)
These courses were taken outside of your undergraduate career and do not count toward your degree.
 
Notes/Restrictions/Limitations (RG5394)
1. The information contained in this analysis should be used to guide you.
2. It should not take the place of consultation with an academic counselor in the Silver Center, Room 905. 
3. You are responsible for checking the Bulletin and with your advisor to make sure you are following all school policies.
4. Should there be any errors in this report, please discuss them with your advisor during your next advising appointment.
5. See appropriate department for evaluation of transfer credit that may be applicable to your major/minor.
Please expand the areas below to view limits on the Pass/Fail option, credit from outside CAS, possible course 
exclusions, and the time allowed to complete your degree.
 
 
Pass/Fail Option (R1680/10)
A. No more than 32 units may be taken with the pass/fail grade option.
B. Pass/fail grade option may not be selected for major, minor, or courses taken to satisfy the College Core Curriculum.
C. Students may elect one pass/fail option each term.
 
· Units: 4.00 used
 
Courses Used
Term Subject Catalog Nbr Course Title Grade Units Type
2025 Spr MATH-UA  233 Theory of Probability P 4.00 EN
 
Maximum Credit from Other Schools (R1680/30)
Satisfied: No more than 16 units may be taken outside of CAS (including transfer work).
 
· Units: 14.00 used
 
Courses Used
Term Subject Catalog Nbr Course Title Grade Units Type
2025 Fall ACCT-UB    1 Prin of Financial Acctg A 4.00 EN
2025 Fall MKTG-UB    1 Intro to Marketing A 4.00 EN
2025 Fall MPAJZ-UE   71 Pop/Jazz Piano (Prvt Lessons) A 2.00 EN
2026 Spr MPAJZ-UE   71 Pop/Jazz Piano (Prvt Lessons) 4.00 IP
 
Time Limit (R1680/60)
All requirements must be met within a period of 8 years from the date of matriculation.
 
Graduation Requirements (RG5001)
Satisfied: Complete the minimum requirements for graduation. In addition to the requirements below, students must 
complete their last 32 units while enrolled at CAS.

CORE Foundations of Scientific Inquiry - Physical and Life Science (RG5393)
Satisfied: Complete Physical Science and Life Science.
 
· Courses: 2.00 required, 2.00 used
 
Physical & Life Science Sequence (R1014)
Complete a year long sequence in one area.
A. Biology: BIOL-UA 11, 12 (transfer credit or test credit only)
B. Chemistry: CHEM-UA 101, 102, 103 & 104 or CHEM-UA 125 & 126.
C. Physics: PHYS-UA 11, 12.
D. Physics for majors: 
Physics I - complete: PHYS-UA 91 and 71.
Physics II - complete: PHYS-UA 93 and 72.
 
Physical and Life Science Sequence (Physics) (R1014/50)
Satisfied: Complete 2 courses:
PHYS-UA 11, 12.
 
· Courses: 2.00 required, 2.00 used
 
Courses Used
Term Subject Catalog Nbr Course Title Grade Units Type
2024 Fall PHYS-UA   11 General Physics I TE 4.00 TE
2024 Fall PHYS-UA   12 General Physics II TE 4.00 TE
 
Writing Proficiency (RG31394)

===== PAGE 6 =====
Satisfied: Complete the required Expository Writing courses with a "C" or higher or take the Writing Proficiency Exam. 
Students who do not pass the exam are required to complete EXPOS-UA 13 - Writing Tutorial.

Not Satisfied: Complete the requirements outlined below. Courses with grades below "C" will not count for the major.  
Pass/Fail grade option may not be used for the major.
 
· Courses: 18.00 required, 17.00 used, 1.00 needed

CSCI-UA 101 - Introduction to Computer Science I

CSCI-UA 201 - Computer Systems Org I
CSCI-UA 202 - Computer Systems Org II
CSCI-UA 310 - Basic Algorithms
